meta.defaultDescription: Municipal ski area in Shichinohe, Aomori. Live operations, snow, access, and course information.

home.transitTitle: A local ski hill close to the Shinkansen
home.transitBody: Easy access from Shichinohe-Towada Station on the Tohoku Shinkansen. Choose your experience—from powder runs to family-friendly slopes.
home.planBody: Purpose-built pages for different visit styles.
home.transitImageAlt: Shinkansen and snowy mountains — transit resort imagery

transit-station.title: About 11 minutes from the Shinkansen
transit-station.body: Roughly 10–11 minutes by taxi from Shichinohe-Towada Station on the Tohoku Shinkansen—easy to fit into a wider trip.

tsuta-onsen.title: Tsuta Onsen — a thousand-year secret spring
tsuta-onsen.body: Near the Oirase stream gateway, a historic inn famed as a “thousand-year” hot spring. After skiing, soak in rare spring-fed baths (hours and fees on the official site).

powder-upper.title: Ungroomed upper terrain
powder-upper.body: For riders who want quieter snow. See course details on the resort map.

family-gentle.title: Families & first-timers
family-gentle.body: Gentle slopes, lessons, and night skiing in a welcoming area.

guides.transitPowderSummary: Powder days within easy reach of the station.
guides.transitShinkansenSummary: A short ski trip built around the Tohoku Shinkansen.
guides.beginnersSummary: Gentle runs and tips for families and first-time visitors.

tsuta-onsen.title: Tsuta Onsen Ryokan
tsuta-onsen.lead: A single inn near the Oirase stream gateway in Towada, Aomori. Rare spring water wells up through the bath floor, surrounded by beech forest—a calm stop after a day on snow.
tsuta-onsen.history: Records note a healing hut on this site as early as 1147 (Kyuan 3), per the ryokan’s official site. Long cherished by writers as a “thousand-year” secret spring.

highlights.spring: True spring-fed flow—water rises directly through the bath floor
highlights.baths: Kyuan-no-yu and Izumi-kyo-no-yu, two forest-framed bathing areas
highlights.nature: Close to Tsuta’s ponds and the Oirase valley in southern Hakkoda

facts.dayUseValue: Available—confirm fees, hours, and seasonal closures on the official site
facts.accessValue: Free shuttle from Shichinohe-Towada Station (reservation required). Driving is also possible.

tsuta-onsen.disclaimer: This page is not affiliated with Tsuta Onsen Ryokan. For day-use availability, prices, hours, closures, and shuttle reservations, always check the official website.
tsuta-onsen.sourceNote: Source: {source} (summary based on official listings as of June 2026)
